__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_16__: Ghost revenue detection: how to find zombie MRR — customers who are paying but not using the product — using last-login and usage data, before that revenue evaporates post-close.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_17__: MRR is only real if customers are actually using the product. A surprising number of SaaS businesses carry "zombie MRR" — active subscriptions where the customer has effectively abandoned the tool but hasn't cancelled yet. This revenue looks stable on the P&L but is one invoice away from disappearing.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_19__: Zombie MRR is recurring revenue from accounts that are paying but no longer engaging with the product. These customers haven't churned in the billing sense — the card still gets charged every month — but they've churned in every meaningful sense: no logins, no usage, no support tickets, no expansion. They're paying out of inertia, forgetfulness, or because cancellation is friction-heavy.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_1__: Customers paying but not using your product are zombie MRR. Learn how to detect inactive paid accounts via last-login and usage data before they churn.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_20__: For buyers, zombie MRR is dangerous because it inflates both the headline MRR number and the implied retention rate. A business reporting $80K MRR with 15% zombie MRR is really a $68K MRR business with a retention problem masked by slow cancellation behavior. When those zombies finally cancel — and they will — the churn rate spikes and the buyer is left holding the decay.

__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_61__: Once you've identified inactive accounts, calculate the exposure:
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_63__: = sum of MRR from accounts with 90+ days of inactivity.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_65__: = Zombie MRR ÷ total MRR. Above 10% is concerning; above 20% is a material deal risk.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_67__: = total MRR − Zombie MRR. This is the revenue base you should underwrite the deal against.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_68__: A target reporting $100K MRR with $18K of zombie MRR has an adjusted MRR of $82K. If you're paying a 5× ARR multiple, that's a $1.08M valuation gap — real money that you're paying for revenue that won't be there in 12 months.

__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_73__: If a customer has stopped using the product, why are they still paying? Several structural factors delay cancellation:
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_77__: . Customers locked into annual plans show up as retained even when they've mentally churned. The cancellation happens in a lump at renewal.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_79__: — products that require emailing support, signing a form, or a phone call to cancel retain zombies longer. This is retention by annoyance, not by value.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_81__: — especially common in low-ACCC B2B tools and team-purchased SaaS where the buyer isn't the user. The card keeps getting charged until someone audits expenses.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_83__: — some customers keep paying because they intend to return. Most don't.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_84__: Each of these factors means the zombie MRR has a half-life. It's not permanent revenue. A business whose retention depends on cancellation friction rather than product value is structurally weaker than the numbers suggest — a core theme in our
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_88__: Zombie MRR almost always churns faster after an acquisition. New ownership often changes billing systems, sends renewal notices that prompt customers to re-evaluate, or tightens cancellation flows. We frequently see 30–50% of zombie MRR churn within 6 months of a deal closing — a spike that the buyer blames on "integration issues" but is really just the delayed reckoning.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_89__: Model this into your underwriting. If the target has $15K of zombie MRR, assume 50% of it churns in year one post-close and haircut the revenue projections accordingly.
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_91__: Can you provide last-login dates for all active accounts?
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_92__: What percentage of MRR comes from accounts with no login in the past 90 days?
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_93__: How do customers cancel — self-serve or via support? What's the cancellation funnel?
__TR_inactive-paid-accounts_94__: Has there been any recent change to the cancellation flow or billing system?
